title: Level 492
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE: }}{{Background|file=void.png}}{{VoidedTheme|mono}} {{Notice|body=}} {{Audio|align=center|file=Callofthevoid.mp3|volume=0.75|mono=no}} {{Audio|align=center|file=Whitewave.mp3|volume=0.25|mono=yes|=no|secrettext=LEAK FOR 492 REWRITE}}
For many years, we have conducted many experiments inside levels and levels. We tested everything in every different way, in different levels, to see which one can do the trick, and what not. Sometimes, these experiments end up destroying an entire level, some make it uninhabitable, and a few of them devastate many lives in the Backrooms. But one level was never spoken to the world ever. We never talked about anything related to Social Experiments, because we never did. However, this level is worse than Social Experiment, something you will also be afraid of. As we know, the concept of Nullxiety has never been done before, because everyone, including us, cannot create it, thus leaving this concept to ashes. But at our hands, we know what these things are. ----
> BACKOS-Version 1.1 | Copyright By Backrooms Technician Industry | Startup Initiated <
> Undergoing Tests <
> Memory Test: 50000 OK <
> RAM Test: 100000 OK <
> Your PC is good to go < - Type a command - ----
Nullsolation is what we call it. Sure, nothing scary here, but remember, you cannot escape from this level, and the thing is, the hallways repeat, over and over, like a recursion. It starts as dark as [[Level 6]], but then adrenaline kicks in, making you realize what's going on. Then you try to run into the end of the hallway, but no matter how much you run, the distance from your starting point to your location will always be the same. Not yet, your organs combust inside you, but you can't feel it, even if your brain dies, you keep running, and then you perish. It isn't our fault that everyone dies here, but there is nothing to start from, nor an end point to end it all. Plus everyone is isolated here so we couldn't retrieve many files for it, so we had to delete the file. Your brain cannot comprehend what's happening, and that's a bad thing. ----
< Load:File/Document492/Txt(ForceLoad) >
> ForceLoad Initiated <
> Loading File/Document492/Txt: 1% <
> Error: Failed to load file/Reason: File deleted by administrator < - Type a command - ----
No, we aren't pretending this is too scary for a kid to know, but the description above is inaccurate. Who knows what is in there if we keep on gaslighting ourselves? Nobody has ever been able to get out alive, not even once. We researched for many years, and yet we kept this level restricted to the world. If it does get leaked out, it is all over. If we had not kept Nullsolation a secret, the Backrooms wouldn't be the same. They would instead be an abandoned hellscape, forever surrounded by nothing but ashes in the endless, dreaded levels. This place would remain silent for the rest of its existence. So many people wanted to take our slot, only to perish in an unfamiliar place. ----
< End.System(Type/Shutdown) >
> Shutting Down System <
> Thank you for using BackOS < ----
But will the void beckon us again? Or will it forever turn a blind eye to us? Then it struck us like a meteor. This level does and does not exist at the same time, staying in a quantum superposition. In this case, one person would be suffering for eternity while the other sees nothing but a crazy man. Such thing cannot happen in the Backrooms, even if regular laws of physics are impossible here. Even the quantum physicists knows nothing about this level and its superposition. Nothing about this level. [[File:Nullxiety1.png|center|750px|link=]]
{{Collapse|initial=...|final=!!!|body= {{Collapse|num=2|initial=Author: ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ |final=|body=Wait a minute... The origins of this place remain a mystery, but it became evident that it existed even before the backrooms were established. This ignited a frenzy to locate this level, leading to chaos in the midst of it all. Countless lives were lost in the quest for this enigmatic level, which embodies the concept of Nullxiety. As war erupted and the world collapsed, some resisted, some fled, and others resigned to their fate. We had to halt our experiments to escape the conflict, a war so vast that only a quarter of us survived. They easily fall victim to various schemes, yet their deception is astute. Even [[When Reality Strikes]] repeated countless times pales in comparison to the many lives they have taken from us. This cycle continues endlessly... In the end... It was a War Without Reason.
